# QA Tracking Report — Unified Fix Plan

**Created:** 2026-03-12  
**Sources:** [March 10 internal QA report](./qa-report-2026-03-10.md) + March 11 GPT first-time-user QA report (attached to GitHub issue)  
**Branch:** `copilot/handle-quirks-and-issues`  
**Purpose:** Living document — tracks every identified issue, its priority, the fix plan, and current status. Update the Status column as each sub-PR is merged.

> **Status key:** 🔴 Open · 🟡 In Progress · ✅ Fixed · ⚠️ Partially Fixed · 🚫 Deferred

---

## Agent workflow — push constraint

Sub-agent sessions (spawned via the `task` tool) **cannot push to GitHub**. The `report_progress` tool is only available to the root agent and always pushes to `copilot/handle-quirks-and-issues`.

**Correct workflow for each sub-PR:**
1. Root agent creates branch: `git checkout -b bugfix/sub-pr-N-...`
2. Sub-agent (or root agent) codes + validates (`yarn lint && yarn build && yarn test`) — **no push attempt**
3. Root agent merges the finished branch into `copilot/handle-quirks-and-issues`
4. Root agent calls `report_progress` to push
5. Repo owner opens PR from `copilot/handle-quirks-and-issues` → `master` (or reviews individual commits per group)

**Sub-agents must NOT call `report_progress` or `git push`** — these will fail with 403.

---

## Executive Summary

Two QA sessions (one automated internal session on March 10, one first-time-user GPT session on March 11) were merged into this single tracking document. Combined findings: **1 High**, **5 Medium**, **9 Low** severity bugs, plus **2 features/investigations** surfaced by the first-time-user session.

### What was working well (both reports agreed)

- Help documentation is clear and comprehensive
- Custom team creation, validation, and random generation are strong
- Automated gameplay flow and Manager Mode prompts feel polished
- Export/import of teams and saves works end-to-end
- Drag-and-drop lineup/bench reordering works correctly

### Top problems (combined)

| Rank | Issue | Severity | Source |
| ---- | ----- | -------- | ------ |
| 1 | Loading a save for a deleted team shows raw team ID in tabs and hit log | High | March 10 |
| 2 | Hit log shows `#N` slot number instead of player name | Medium | March 10 |
| 3 | `useRxdbGameSync: failed to update progress (game over)` console error | Medium | March 10 |
| 4 | "Resume Current Game" shown after a FINAL game | Medium | March 10 + March 11 |
| 5 | Save import error leaks the word "undefined" | Medium | March 10 |
| 6 | No starter / demo teams — new users cannot play immediately | High UX | March 11 |
| 7 | Load Saved Game dedicated page showed empty even when saves exist | Medium UX | March 11 |
| 8 | Career Stats page empty after completing a game | Medium UX | March 11 |

---

## Issue Register

### Sub-PR 1 — Group 2: Bugs A + J *(ship together — same rendering area)*

**PR:** [#171 — Fix hit log showing #N slot and raw team ID for orphaned saves](https://github.com/maniator/blipit-legends/pull/171) · 🟡 Open · lint ✅ · build ✅ · E2E running

| ID | Severity | Title | Source | Status |
| -- | -------- | ----- | ------ | ------ |
| Bug J | 🔴 High | Loading a save for a deleted team shows raw team ID in tabs and hit log | March 10 | 🟡 In Progress |
| Bug A | 🔵 Medium | Hit log shows `#N` position number instead of player name | March 10 | 🟡 In Progress |

**Files changed:**
- `src/features/gameplay/context/index.tsx` — add `batterName?: string` to `PlayLogEntry`
- `src/features/gameplay/context/hitBall.ts` — populate `batterName` from active lineup at time of hit
- `src/features/gameplay/components/HitLog/index.tsx` — use `teamLabels[entry.team]` instead of `resolveTeamLabel` (fixes Bug J); render `entry.batterName ?? \`#${entry.batterNum}\`` (fixes Bug A)

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Hit log entries show batter name (e.g. `"▲3 — Roberto Martinez"`), not `#4`
- [ ] Backward-compatible: older saves without `batterName` fall back to `#N`
- [ ] Loading a save for a deleted team shows the original team name (from `teamLabels`), not a raw ID

### Sub-PR 2 — Bug C + Bug 3 *(related: both concern the game-over state)*

**PR:** [#172 — Fix Resume button persisting after FINAL game + suppress redundant RxDB updateProgress on final load](https://github.com/maniator/blipit-legends/pull/172) · 🟡 Open · lint ✅ · build ✅ · E2E running

| ID | Severity | Title | Source | Status |
| -- | -------- | ----- | ------ | ------ |
| Bug C | 🔵 Medium | "Resume Current Game" shown on home screen after a FINAL game | March 10 + March 11 | 🟡 In Progress |
| Bug 3 | 🔵 Medium | `useRxdbGameSync: failed to update progress (game over)` console error | March 10 | 🟡 In Progress |

**Files changed (Bug C):**
- `src/storage/types.ts` — add `onGameOver: () => void` to `AppShellOutletContext`
- `src/features/gameplay/components/AppShell/index.tsx` — add `handleGameOver` callback that resets `hasActiveSession` to `false`; expose via outlet context
- `src/features/gameplay/components/Game/GameInner.tsx` — call `onGameOver` prop when `gameOver` transitions to `true`
- `src/features/gameplay/pages/GamePage/index.tsx` — thread `onGameOver` from outlet context down to `<Game>`

**Files changed (Bug 3):**
- `src/features/saves/hooks/useRxdbGameSync.ts` — accept `wasAlreadyFinalOnLoad` param; guard game-over `updateProgress` call when `wasAlreadyFinalOnLoad === true`

> ⚠️ `saveStore.ts` is intentionally **not** changed here to keep this sub-PR conflict-free with Sub-PR 3. The `updateProgress` silent-resolve guard ships in Sub-PR 3 alongside the other `saveStore.ts` changes.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] After a game reaches FINAL naturally, the home screen does NOT show a Resume button
- [ ] After loading a FINAL save and navigating home, no Resume button
- [ ] For an in-progress game, Resume button still appears and works
- [ ] No console error fires when a game ends naturally
- [ ] No console error fires when loading a FINAL save and navigating home
- [ ] No console error fires when clicking Resume for a FINAL game

### Sub-PR 3 — Group 3: Bugs F + 5 *(ship together — same "undefined" error category)*

**PR:** [#173 — Fix "undefined" leaking in save/team import errors + updateProgress silent-resolve guard](https://github.com/maniator/blipit-legends/pull/173) · 🟡 Open · lint ✅ · build ✅ · E2E running

| ID | Severity | Title | Source | Status |
| -- | -------- | ----- | ------ | ------ |

| Bug 5 | 🟡 Low | Team import error leaks the word "undefined" | March 10 | 🟡 In Progress |

- `src/features/saves/storage/saveStore.ts` — (1) guard `version` type before comparing in `importRxdbSave`; emit user-friendly message when `version` is absent or not a number; (2) make `updateProgress` silently resolve (not throw) when the target save doc no longer exists *(moved here from Sub-PR 2 to keep file ownership clean)*
- `src/features/customTeams/storage/customTeamExportImport.ts` — guard `formatVersion` type before comparing; emit user-friendly message with no raw tokens

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Pasting `{}` into save import shows a clean message with no `undefined`/`null`/`NaN`
- [ ] Pasting `{}` into team import shows a clean message with no `undefined`/`null`/`NaN`
- [ ] Valid imports still succeed without errors

### Sub-PR 6 — March 11 Priority 1: Demo / starter teams

**PR:** [#176 — Add hardcoded demo teams for new users](https://github.com/maniator/blipit-legends/pull/176) · 🟡 Draft / WIP

| ID | Severity | Title | Source | Status |
| -- | -------- | ----- | ------ | ------ |
| New-1 | 🔴 High UX | No starter teams — new users cannot play immediately | March 11 | 🟡 In Progress |

**Context:** When a brand-new user visits the app, they must create at least two custom teams before they can play. This is the single biggest first-time-user friction point identified in the March 11 report.

**Files changed:**
- `src/features/customTeams/generation/demoTeams.ts` *(new)* — two fully-populated hardcoded team definitions with real-looking names, cities, and stat distributions
- `src/shared/hooks/useSeedDemoTeams.ts` *(new)* — hook: on first load, if `customTeams` collection is empty, inserts the two demo teams via `CustomTeamStore.createCustomTeam`
- `src/features/gameplay/components/RootLayout/index.tsx` — call `useSeedDemoTeams()` so the check runs once on every first app load *(RootLayout used instead of AppShell to avoid a merge conflict with Sub-PR 2)*

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] A brand-new user (empty IndexedDB) sees two teams already present when they open Manage Teams
- [ ] New Game works immediately for a brand-new user without any manual team creation
- [ ] Users who already have custom teams see no change (hook is a no-op when teams exist)
- [ ] Demo teams are fully valid (pass all schema/stat validation)

## Investigations (March 11 report — not confirmed code bugs)

### Inv-1: Load Saved Game page shows empty when saves exist in the in-game modal

**Status:** 🔴 Needs investigation  
**Context:** The March 11 tester found the `/saves` page showed "No saves yet" even though saves were visible in the in-game 💾 modal. Both surfaces use the same underlying `getDb()` singleton and should see identical data. The most likely explanations are a test-environment artifact (different browser session) or a timing issue on first navigation to `/saves` before DB is ready.  
**Action:** Verify reproducibility. If confirmed: investigate whether `getDb()` can return a different instance than what RxDatabaseProvider uses inside the game, or whether there is a timing window during first initialization. If not reproducible: document as environment-specific and close.

### Inv-2: Career Stats page empty after completing a full game

**Status:** 🔴 Needs investigation  
**Context:** The March 11 tester saw "No completed games yet for this team" on the `/stats` page after playing a full exhibition game. The in-game Career tab (computed from the live play log) showed stats correctly. `useGameHistorySync` should commit stats to RxDB when `gameOver` becomes true, using `gameInstanceId` as the primary key.  
**Action:** Verify reproducibility. If confirmed: check whether `gameOver` is true at commit time, whether `gameInstanceId` is populated, and whether `commitCompletedGame` is throwing silently. Add explicit error logging if needed. Note: Bug 3 (wave 2) fixes a console error that fires after game-over, and the `wasAlreadyFinalOnLoad` fix may also affect this flow — re-test career stats after Sub-PR 2 is merged.

## Implementation order

Work top-to-bottom. Each sub-PR is independent unless noted.

All six sub-PRs touch disjoint sets of files and can be run fully in parallel.

| Sub-PR | Bugs | Files owned exclusively |
| ------ | ---- | ---------------------- |
| 1 — Bugs A + J | Hit log names + orphan team ID | `context/index.tsx`, `hitBall.ts`, `HitLog/index.tsx`, `HitLog/hitLog.test.tsx` |
| 2 — Bug C + Bug 3 | Resume button + console error | `storage/types.ts`, `AppShell/index.tsx`, `GameInner.tsx`, `GamePage/index.tsx`, `useRxdbGameSync.ts` |
| 3 — Bugs F + 5 | Import error "undefined" leaks + `updateProgress` guard | `saveStore.ts`, `customTeamExportImport.ts` |
| 4 — Bugs B + G | Save delete confirm + back button | `SaveSlotList/index.tsx`, `PlayerCareerPage/index.tsx` |
| 5 — Bugs D + E + H + I | HelpContent copy fixes | `HelpContent/index.tsx` |
| 6 — New-1 | Demo teams for new users | `demoTeams.ts` *(new)*, `useSeedDemoTeams.ts` *(new)*, `RootLayout/index.tsx` |

## Code Review: PR #171

**PR:** [#171 — Fix hit log showing #N slot and raw team ID for orphaned saves](https://github.com/maniator/blipit-legends/pull/171)  
**Branch:** `copilot/copilotfix-bug-a-j` → `copilot/handle-quirks-and-issues`  
**Review date:** 2026-03-12  
**Reviewer:** Copilot coding agent  
**Verdict:** ✅ **Ready to merge** — pending E2E run completion

### CI Status

| Check | Result |
| ----- | ------ |
| lint | ✅ Pass |
| build | ✅ Pass |
| E2E (chromium shard 1) | 🔄 In progress |
| E2E (chromium shard 2) | 🔄 In progress |
| E2E (chromium shard 3) | ✅ Pass |
| E2E (webkit shard 1–3) | 🔄 In progress |
| Update snapshots (chromium) | ✅ Pass |

### Files changed (5)

1. `src/features/gameplay/context/index.tsx` — +2 lines
2. `src/features/gameplay/context/hitBall.ts` — +16 lines
3. `src/features/gameplay/components/HitLog/index.tsx` — +3 / -10 lines
4. `src/features/gameplay/components/HitLog/hitLog.test.tsx` — net rewrite (simpler + 3 new tests)
5. `.github/docs/qa-report-2026-03-12.md` — tracking doc update

### Change-by-change review

#### `PlayLogEntry` — `batterName?: string` added (`context/index.tsx`)

✅ Field is optional — fully backward compatible with older saves that lack it.  
✅ JSDoc comment explains the fallback behavior clearly.  
No concerns.

#### `hitBall.ts` — populate `batterName` at hit time

✅ Resolution order is correct:
1. `playerOverrides[team][playerId]?.nickname?.trim()` — custom team player nickname
2. `generateRoster(base.teams[battingTeam]).batters.find(p => p.id === playerId)?.name` — stock team player name

✅ Both paths guard `playerId` being absent before accessing.  
✅ Spread `...(batterName ? { batterName } : {})` keeps the entry compact when unresolvable.

**One observation (not blocking):** `generateRoster` is called on every hit for stock teams to resolve the batter name. For custom teams with a nickname this is skipped (short-circuit), but for stock-team hits it generates all 9 batters + pitchers just to find one name. This is deterministic and fast (no I/O) so it's not a correctness or performance concern in practice — it's worth noting for a future optimization if `generateRoster` becomes more expensive.

**Edge case correctly handled:** Custom team players without a nickname and non-stock teams produce `batterName = undefined`, falling back to `#N` in the UI — this is acceptable.

#### `HitLog/index.tsx` — use `teamLabels`, render `batterLabel`

✅ Removes the `useCustomTeams` async hook entirely — team labels now come from `teamLabels` in `GameContext`, which are pre-resolved at game-start/restore. This means deleted-team saves always show the original name.  
✅ `const batterLabel = entry.batterName ?? \`#${entry.batterNum}\`` is clean and handles the backward-compat case.  
✅ Render change: `{teamLabels[entry.team]} {batterLabel}` — the `#` prefix is now only prepended for slot-number fallbacks (when `batterName` absent), not for named entries. This is correct behavior.

#### `hitLog.test.tsx` — simplified + 3 new tests

✅ Removes the 50-line `vi.mock("@shared/hooks/useCustomTeams", ...)` block — no longer needed.  
✅ Removes the hand-rolled `makeCtx` — replaced by `makeContextValue` from `@test/testHelpers`, matching the codebase convention.  
✅ `makeContextValue({ teamLabels: ["Away FC", "Home SC"], ...overrides })` — correct usage.  
✅ Three new tests cover:
  - `batterName` renders when present, `#N` does not appear
  - `#N` fallback when `batterName` absent
  - `teamLabels` used for orphaned-team saves (not raw team ID)

One test updates the existing "shows 1B and Away team label" assertion from `/Away #3/` to `/Away FC/` — this is correct given the new context shape.

### Acceptance criteria check

| Criterion | Status |
| --------- | ------ |
| Hit log entries show batter name (e.g. `"▲3 — Roberto Martinez"`) | ✅ Implemented |
| Backward compat: older saves without `batterName` fall back to `#N` | ✅ `batterName ?? \`#N\`` |
| Loading a save for a deleted team shows original team name (not raw ID) | ✅ `teamLabels[entry.team]` |

### Summary

The implementation is correct, clean, and well-tested. The change is surgical — 5 files, ~125 additions / ~123 deletions (mostly test cleanup). No concerns blocking merge. The only non-blocking observation is that `generateRoster` is called at hit-time for stock teams; this is acceptable given current performance characteristics.

**Recommend merging once all E2E shards complete (expected passing based on the shard-3 result).**

## Agent prompts

Copy-paste these prompts when spawning a Copilot coding agent for each sub-PR.
Each prompt is self-contained — read the spec files it references before starting.

---

### Sub-PR 1 prompt — Bugs A + J (hit log player names + orphan team ID)

```
You are working on the repository maniator/blipit-legends.
Branch to work on: copilot/handle-quirks-and-issues

Fix Bug A and Bug J together (same rendering area — ship as one commit).

Full specs:
- .github/docs/qa-2026-03-10/bug-a-hit-log-player-names.md
- .github/docs/qa-2026-03-10/bug-j-orphan-save-raw-team-id.md

## Bug A — Hit log shows #N slot number instead of player name
File: src/features/gameplay/context/index.tsx
  - Add optional field `batterName?: string` to the `PlayLogEntry` type.

File: src/features/gameplay/context/hitBall.ts
  - In `processConfirmedHit`, after `const batterNum = batterSlotIdx + 1;`:
    Resolve the batter's display name at hit time so it is stored in the log.
    Resolution order: (1) playerOverrides nickname (trimmed, non-empty), (2) roster-generated
    name via `generateRoster(base.teams[battingTeam]).batters.find(p => p.id === playerId)?.name`.
    If neither resolves, leave `batterName` undefined.
    Spread `...(batterName ? { batterName } : {})` into the `PlayLogEntry` object appended
    to `playLog`.

File: src/features/gameplay/components/HitLog/index.tsx
  - Compute `const batterLabel = entry.batterName ?? \`#\${entry.batterNum}\`` for each entry.
  - Render `batterLabel` in place of the current `#\${entry.batterNum}` literal.

## Bug J — Orphan team shows raw ID (e.g. ct_Ld6X1) in hit log
File: src/features/gameplay/components/HitLog/index.tsx
  - Read `teamLabels` from `useGameContext()` instead of calling `resolveTeamLabel`.
  - Remove the `useCustomTeams` hook import and usage entirely.
  - Use `teamLabels[entry.team]` as the team name label.

## Tests
File: src/features/gameplay/components/HitLog/hitLog.test.tsx
  - Remove the `vi.mock("@shared/hooks/useCustomTeams", ...)` block (no longer needed).
  - Replace the hand-rolled `makeCtx` with `makeContextValue` from `@test/testHelpers`,
    passing `teamLabels: ["Away FC", "Home SC"]` as override.
  - Update the existing "shows 1B and Away team label" assertion to match `Away FC` (from
    teamLabels) not `Away #3`.
  - Add test: "shows batterName when present instead of #N slot" — entry with
    `batterNum: 3, batterName: "Roberto Martinez"` → text includes "Roberto Martinez",
    does NOT include "#3".
  - Add test: "falls back to #N slot when batterName is absent" — entry without batterName →
    text includes "#4".
  - Add test: "uses teamLabels for orphaned-team saves" — teams: ["custom:ct_deleted",
    "custom:ct_home"], teamLabels: ["Buffalo Dynamo", "Cincinnati Bucks"], entry team 0 →
    text includes "Buffalo Dynamo", does NOT include "ct_deleted".

## Validation
Run: yarn lint && yarn build && yarn test
All must pass with zero errors before considering this done.
```

### Sub-PR 2 prompt — Bug C + Bug 3 (resume button after final + console error)

```
You are working on the repository maniator/blipit-legends.
Branch to work on: copilot/handle-quirks-and-issues

Fix Bug C and Bug 3 together (both concern the game-over state — ship as one commit).

Full specs:
- .github/docs/qa-2026-03-10/bug-c-resume-button-after-final.md
- .github/docs/qa-2026-03-10/bug-03-rxdb-game-sync-error.md

## Bug C — "Resume Current Game" shown on home screen after a FINAL game

The home screen shows a "Resume" button when `hasActiveSession` is true in AppShell.
After a game reaches FINAL it must be cleared so the Resume button disappears.

File: src/storage/types.ts
  - Add `onGameOver: () => void` to the `AppShellOutletContext` interface.

File: src/features/gameplay/components/AppShell/index.tsx
  - Add a `handleGameOver` callback: `() => setHasActiveSession(false)`.
  - Expose it on the outlet context: `{ ..., onGameOver: handleGameOver }`.

File: src/features/gameplay/components/Game/GameInner.tsx (or wherever the `gameOver`
  transition is observed inside the Game component tree):
  - Accept an `onGameOver?: () => void` prop.
  - Call `onGameOver()` in a `useEffect` that fires when `gameOver` transitions to `true`.
    Guard with a ref so it fires at most once per game instance.

File: src/features/gameplay/pages/GamePage/index.tsx
  - Destructure `onGameOver` from the outlet context.
  - Pass it down to `<Game onGameOver={onGameOver} />`.

## Bug 3 — useRxdbGameSync console error on game over

File: src/features/saves/hooks/useRxdbGameSync.ts
  - Add a `wasAlreadyFinalOnLoad: boolean` parameter.
  - When `gameOver` becomes true AND `wasAlreadyFinalOnLoad` is true, skip the
    `updateProgress` call entirely (the save is already final — re-marking it is both
    redundant and the source of the error).

Wire `wasAlreadyFinalOnLoad` from the load path: wherever a saved game is loaded and
`gameOver` is already true in the loaded state, pass `wasAlreadyFinalOnLoad: true` to the
hook.

NOTE: Do NOT touch `src/features/saves/storage/saveStore.ts` in this sub-PR.
The `updateProgress` silent-resolve guard is handled in Sub-PR 3 to keep file
ownership clean and enable all sub-PRs to run in parallel.

## Validation
Run: yarn lint && yarn build && yarn test
All must pass with zero errors before considering this done.
```

---

### Sub-PR 3 prompt — Bugs F + 5 (import error "undefined" leaks)

```
You are working on the repository maniator/blipit-legends.
Branch to work on: copilot/handle-quirks-and-issues

Fix Bug F, Bug 5, and the saveStore.updateProgress guard together — all changes are
isolated to saveStore.ts and customTeamExportImport.ts. This sub-PR is fully
parallel-safe with all other sub-PRs (no shared files).

Full specs:
- .github/docs/qa-2026-03-10/bug-f-save-import-undefined.md
- .github/docs/qa-2026-03-10/bug-05-team-import-undefined.md

## Bug F — Save import error leaks "undefined"

File: src/features/saves/storage/saveStore.ts  (function: importRxdbSave)
  - Before the version comparison, check `typeof parsed.version !== "number"`.
  - If true, throw a clear user-facing error: `"Invalid save file: missing or unrecognised
    format. Please export a save from the app and try again."` — no raw token values.
  - Existing valid-version path is unchanged.

## saveStore.updateProgress silent-resolve guard (moved here from Sub-PR 2)

File: src/features/saves/storage/saveStore.ts  (function: updateProgress)
  - If the target save document is not found (RxDB returns null/undefined), silently
    resolve instead of throwing. Log a debug message if desired but do not throw.
  - This prevents the `useRxdbGameSync: failed to update progress` console error when
    the save is deleted between game start and game over.

## Bug 5 — Team import error leaks "undefined"

File: src/features/customTeams/storage/customTeamExportImport.ts  (function: parseExportedCustomTeams or equivalent)
  - Before the formatVersion comparison, check `typeof parsed.formatVersion !== "number"`.
  - If true, throw a clear user-facing error: `"Invalid team file: missing or unrecognised
    format. Please export a team from Manage Teams and try again."` — no raw token values.
  - Existing valid-version path is unchanged.

## Tests
For both files, add a unit test that:
  1. Calls the import function with `{}` (empty object).
  2. Expects the thrown error message to NOT contain the strings "undefined", "null", or "NaN".
  3. Expects the thrown error message to contain "Invalid" and "format".

## Validation
Run: yarn lint && yarn build && yarn test
All must pass with zero errors before considering this done.
```

### Sub-PR 4 prompt — Bugs B + G (save delete confirmation + back button)

```
You are working on the repository maniator/blipit-legends.
Branch to work on: copilot/handle-quirks-and-issues

Fix Bug B and Bug G — two standalone low-risk changes, ship as one commit.

Full specs:
- .github/docs/qa-2026-03-10/bug-b-save-delete-no-confirm.md
- .github/docs/qa-2026-03-10/bug-g-back-button-navigation.md

## Bug B — Save deletion has no confirmation dialog

File: src/features/saves/components/SaveSlotList/index.tsx
  - Find the delete button handler that calls `onDelete(s.id)` (or equivalent).
  - Wrap it with a guard: only call `onDelete` if
    `window.confirm("Delete this save? This cannot be undone.")` returns true.
  - Match the existing confirmation pattern already used for team deletion elsewhere in the app.

## Bug G — ← Back on player career page uses browser history instead of /stats

File: src/features/careerStats/pages/PlayerCareerPage/index.tsx
  - Find the back navigation handler (currently `navigate(-1)` or equivalent).
  - Replace with explicit navigation:
    If `teamParam` (the `?team=` query param) is present: navigate to
    `/stats?team=\${encodeURIComponent(teamParam)}`.
    Otherwise: navigate to `/stats`.
  - This ensures Back always lands on the stats page regardless of browser history.

## Tests
- For Bug B: add or update the SaveSlotList test to assert that window.confirm is called
  before onDelete when the delete button is clicked. Use `vi.spyOn(window, "confirm")`.
- For Bug G: add or update the PlayerCareerPage test to assert that navigate is called with
  `/stats` (not `-1`) when Back is clicked, both with and without a teamParam.

## Validation
Run: yarn lint && yarn build && yarn test
All must pass with zero errors before considering this done.
```

### Sub-PR 6 prompt — New-1 (demo / starter teams for new users)

```
You are working on the repository maniator/blipit-legends.
Branch to work on: copilot/handle-quirks-and-issues

Add two hardcoded demo teams so brand-new users can play immediately without
having to create teams first.

Context: When a brand-new user opens the app for the first time (empty IndexedDB),
there are no custom teams and the New Game form has no options to pick — they cannot
play at all. Seeding two starter teams on first launch fixes this.

## Files to create

### src/features/customTeams/generation/demoTeams.ts  (new file)
Export a const `DEMO_TEAMS: DemoTeamDef[]` (define the type inline) — two teams:

Team 1 — "Riverside Rockets"
  - city: "Riverside", name: "Rockets", abbreviation: "RIV"
  - 9 lineup players + 4 bench players + 4 pitchers
  - Each hitter: contact, power, speed each in range 40–70, sum ≤ 150 (HITTER_STAT_CAP)
  - Each pitcher: velocity, control, stamina each in range 40–70, sum ≤ 160 (PITCHER_STAT_CAP)
  - Use a variety of realistic names (first + last, American English)

Team 2 — "Lakewood Legends"
  - city: "Lakewood", name: "Legends", abbreviation: "LAK"
  - Same roster structure and stat constraints as Team 1

Each player needs: name (string), role ("hitter" | "pitcher"), batting stats (if hitter),
pitching stats (if pitcher). Do NOT include IDs — those are generated by the store.

### src/shared/hooks/useSeedDemoTeams.ts  (new file)
A React hook (useSeedDemoTeams) that:
  1. Reads the customTeams collection count from the RxDB store (via useCustomTeams or
     directly from the store singleton).
  2. On mount, if count === 0, calls `CustomTeamStore.createCustomTeam(...)` for each
     team definition in DEMO_TEAMS.
  3. Is a no-op if teams already exist (count > 0).
  4. Runs at most once — use a ref guard so it does not re-run on re-renders.

## File to modify

### src/features/gameplay/components/RootLayout/index.tsx
  - Import and call `useSeedDemoTeams()` inside the component body.
  - RootLayout wraps every route (via `<Outlet />`), so the seeding check runs once on
    every app start without conflicting with Sub-PR 2, which modifies AppShell.

## Validation rules
  - Demo teams must pass all existing CustomTeamStore validation (stat clamp 0–100 per
    stat, sum within caps, required fields present).
  - `yarn lint && yarn build && yarn test` must all pass with zero errors.
  - Do not hardcode player IDs — let `createCustomTeam` generate them.
```
